actionable_feedback: Open cubesat_power_budget.xlsx and confirm that each cited cell (Inputs!C4..C10 and Calc!C4..C20) contains the formula expression shown in ops_note, not a hard-coded numeric value. Record any cells that are values instead of formulas.

Populate the missing Inputs cells (Inputs!C13..C17) with explicit entries: battery round-trip efficiency (%), initial battery SoC (Wh or %), battery EOL capacity (Wh), solar-array degradation margin (%), and mode duty-cycle granularity. If unknown, add conservative defaults and mark them as 'assumed' with provenance.

Add a 'Formulas' or 'CalcExport' sheet that either (a) contains plain-text formula strings for the key calculation cells (Calc!C4..C20) or (b) export the calculation rows as CSV with formula text included. This enables automated semantic verification where openpyxl is unavailable.

In the sensitivity sheet, change the perturbation calculations to reference base input cells (e.g., use formulas like =Inputs!C4*(1+perturbation)) and document whether sunlit duration is held constant when eclipse duration is perturbed.

Update ops_note.md: (i) include a short justification for the assumptions (why DoD from full battery is appropriate), (ii) include a 'how to close' checklist listing exactly what a verifier should inspect in the workbook, and (iii) attach or reference the exported CSV/formulas sheet or screenshots (if allowed).

Re-run the validation checks after making the workbook changes and include the updated ops_note and exported formula/CSV artifacts in the revised attempt so automated/verifier checks can re-evaluate semantic correctness.
feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables are complete and numerically consistent per ops_note.md; DoD check correctly reports failure (33.83% > 30%). The main outstanding verification issue is that the grader could not parse the .xlsx programmatically, so a human verifier must confirm that cited Calc! and Inputs! cells contain formulas (not hard-coded values). Also add missing battery-related inputs and make formulas/exports available to enable automated checks.

limitations: openpyxl/xlsx parsing unavailable in grading environment; assessment relied on ops_note.md content and artifact presence.
Human verifier should open the workbook to confirm formula text vs. hard-coded values.
recommended_verifier_checks: Inspect formula text for Calc!C4..C20 and Inputs!C4..C10 to confirm live formulas.
Confirm missing Inputs entries were added or justified in the revised attempt.
Verify the included Formulas/CalcExport sheet or CSV contains correct formula strings matching ops_note arithmetic.
